title: podman system migrate stops pods but doesn't start them
body:
### Issue Description

podman system migrate stopped all containers but doesn't restart them

### Steps to reproduce the issue

`podman system migrate`

### Describe the results you received

No running containers

### Describe the results you expected

running containers

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Arch linux

### Additional information

`systemctl --user restart podman-restart.service`
error
`Failed to start Podman Start All Containers With Restart Policy Set To Always.`

I use the policy `OnFailure` and added the pods to systemD with
`systemctl --user enable --now podman-kube@$(systemd-escape $(pwd)/ci-pod.yaml).service`

The generated service files looking like
```
[Unit]
Description=A template for running K8s workloads via podman-kube-play
Documentation=man:podman-kube-play(1)
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman kube play --replace --service-container=true %I
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman kube down %I
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

comments.body:
I wouldn't consider this a bug, the man page clearly mentions that:
>  Instead  of  doing  it manually,  podman  system migrate can be used to stop both the running containers and the pause process. 

We could consider this as feature but the problem is this is really not trivial to implement. podman runs in a user namespace and migrate should create a new one killing the current one. Because the current process still is in the old user namespace it is impossible to start the containers from there and once you joined a user namespace you cannot join the host user namespace again to make the new, thus we have the current behaviour.

couldn't the user podman restart service get used to start the pods?

I don't really understand why the restart service dies if at least one of my pods is not running.
I mean of course it shouldn't start manually stopped containers, if they use `OnFailure`, but also it shouldn't error.
And if the error happens, it also stops every other running pod.

Also since there is no auto completion on (re)start but only on status in systemctl it is painfully to start every container without a system restart.

```
Feb 15 12:20:45 hostname podman[8124]: 2023-02-15 12:20:45.584442303 +0100 CET m=+0.300673393 container init 768a509af930db0abeb37e42b2dc96abc67441726d3bb35deef2dfe43f3fd9>
Feb 15 12:20:45 hostname podman[8124]: 2023-02-15 12:20:45.673581577 +0100 CET m=+0.389812666 container start 768a509af930db0abeb37e42b2dc96abc67441726d3bb35deef2dfe43f3fd>
Feb 15 12:20:47 hostname podman[8225]: 2023-02-15 12:20:47.670878914 +0100 CET m=+1.908053999 container cleanup 768a509af930db0abeb37e42b2dc96abc67441726d3bb35deef2dfe43f3>
Feb 15 12:20:47 hostname podman[8124]: Error: container ca9080f25f8aaceed3466662af0695906f1195e1a1b575d2f24a6da2c10ca1d6 does not exist in database: no such container
Feb 15 12:20:48 hostname podman[8225]: 2023-02-15 12:20:48.234094569 +0100 CET m=+2.471269662 pod stop b1bbf72b03efe46f375970d10e59bd88a119b641d8ce07fa67919056ad10722c (im>
Feb 15 12:20:48 hostname podman[8124]: 2023-02-15 12:20:48.360800806 +0100 CET m=+3.077031898 pod stop b1bbf72b03efe46f375970d10e59bd88a119b641d8ce07fa67919056ad10722c (im>
Feb 15 12:20:49 hostname systemd[2444]: podman-restart.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
Feb 15 12:20:49 hostname systemd[2444]: podman-restart.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Feb 15 12:20:49 hostname systemd[2444]: Failed to start Podman Start All Containers With Restart Policy Set To Always.
Feb 15 12:20:49 hostname systemd[2444]: podman-restart.service: Triggering OnFailure= dependencies.
```

I don't understand how this is related to podman system migrate?

This looks like a different issue, podman-restart should start your containers once at boot and I don't think using restart on this unit is recommended because it will could races between different podman processes.
Just think about it there could be a conflict between the podman-kube unit and the restart one. In general podman-restart.service should likely ignore containers that are started from other systemd units. cc @vrothberg
podman system migrate should almost never happen ,so doing engineering to restart running containers will never happen..

I'm sorry for drifting into a separate issue.

Ah, I thought it's named restart to restart the containers.

Oh, I thought I should manually run `podman system migrate` after each podman major/minor upgrade, because of the manual https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-system-migrate.1.html
restart containers is to start containers that marked as restart=always at boot time.

Thank you.
It seem's that this service is useless for me if I use systemD and just use an enabled systemD service for each pod.
